quick_answer_bullets: Speed limits are in mph and vary by state
Stop fully for school buses with flashing lights
Right on red is common—except NYC unless signed
Hands-free phone laws differ; check each state

faq.question: When must I stop for a school bus in the US?
faq.question_de: Wann muss ich in den USA für einen Schulbus anhalten?
faq.answer: When a school bus stops and extends its stop sign with red lights flashing, traffic in both directions must stop (unless a physical median divides the road). Passing is a serious offence—fines, licence points, and even jail apply in many states.
faq.answer_de: Wenn ein Schulbus anhält, Stoppschild ausfährt und rote Lichter blinkt, muss der Verkehr in beide Richtungen anhalten (außer bei physischer Mittelinsel). Überholen ist schwerwiegend—Bußgelder, Punkte und sogar Haft in vielen Bundesstaaten.

faq.question: Can I turn right on red in the United States?
faq.question_de: Darf ich in den USA bei Rot rechts abbiegen?
faq.answer: In most states, right turns on red are allowed after a complete stop unless a sign prohibits it. New York City is a major exception—right on red is illegal unless a sign explicitly permits it. Always stop fully and yield to pedestrians.
faq.answer_de: In den meisten Bundesstaaten ist Rechtsabbiegen bei Rot nach vollständigem Stopp erlaubt, sofern kein Verbotsschild steht. New York City ist eine wichtige Ausnahme—Rechts bei Rot ist verboten, außer ein Schild erlaubt es. Immer voll anhalten und Fußgängern Vorfahrt geben.

faq.question: How do speed limits work in the US?
faq.question_de: Wie funktionieren Tempolimits in den USA?
faq.answer: Speed limits are posted in mph, not km/h. Typical defaults are 25 mph in cities, 55 mph on rural two-lane roads, and 65–70 mph on interstates—but Texas and Utah allow up to 80–85 mph on some routes. Always follow posted signs.
faq.answer_de: Tempolimits sind in mph, nicht km/h. Typisch 25 mph in Städten, 55 mph auf Landstraßen und 65–70 mph auf Interstates—Texas und Utah erlauben bis 80–85 mph auf manchen Strecken. Immer Schilder befolgen.

faq.question: Do driving rules vary by state?
faq.answer: Yes. While basics are similar, phone laws, child-seat ages, turn-on-red rules, and fine amounts differ. California requires hands-free phone mounting; Florida requires headlights when wipers are on. Check the state DMV for your route.
faq.answer_de: Ja. Grundlagen sind ähnlich, aber Handygesetze, Kindersitzalter, Abbiegeregeln und Bußgelder unterscheiden sich. Kalifornien verlangt Freisprech-Montage; Florida Licht bei Scheibenwischern. DMV des Bundesstaats für deine Route prüfen.

faq.question: How does a four-way stop work?
faq.answer: At a four-way stop, the first vehicle to come to a complete stop goes first. If two arrive together, yield to the vehicle on your right. Stop means full stop—rolling stops are ticketed.
faq.answer_de: Am Vier-Wege-Stop fährt zuerst, wer zuerst vollständig anhält. Bei gleichzeitiger Ankunft Vorfahrt dem Fahrzeug von rechts. Stopp heißt vollständig anhalten—Rollstopps werden geahndet.

faq.question: What is the Move Over Law?
faq.answer: Most states require you to change lanes away from stopped emergency, tow, or service vehicles with flashing lights. If you cannot move over safely, slow down significantly. Violations carry fines and points.
faq.answer_de: Die meisten Bundesstaaten verlangen, bei haltenden Einsatz-, Abschlepp- oder Servicefahrzeugen mit Blaulicht auszuweichen. Wenn das nicht sicher geht, deutlich langsamer fahren. Verstöße bringen Bußgelder und Punkte.

faq.question: What are California's phone laws while driving?
faq.question_de: Was sind Kaliforniens Handyregeln am Steuer?
faq.answer: California bans holding a phone while driving. Mount the device and use single-swipe hands-free commands. Texting and handheld calls are ticketed; fines increase on repeat offences within 36 months.
faq.answer_de: Kalifornien verbietet Handy in der Hand am Steuer. Gerät montieren und Freisprech-Befehle mit einem Wisch nutzen. SMS und Handanrufe werden geahndet; Wiederholung innerhalb 36 Monate erhöht die Strafe.

faq.question: Must I use headlights in the rain in Florida?
faq.question_de: Muss ich bei Regen in Florida Licht einschalten?
faq.answer: Yes. Florida law requires headlights when wipers are in continuous use due to rain, fog, or smoke. This improves visibility and is enforced during storms.
faq.answer_de: Ja. Floridas Gesetz verlangt Licht, wenn Scheibenwischer wegen Regen, Nebel oder Rauch dauerhaft laufen. Verbessert Sichtbarkeit und wird bei Stürmen kontrolliert.

faq.question: Should I drive in New York City?
faq.question_de: Sollte ich in New York City Auto fahren?
faq.answer: Manhattan driving is stressful and expensive—bridge/tunnel tolls, scarce street parking, and aggressive ticketing. No right on red unless signed. Many visitors use transit, taxis, or park in outer boroughs.
faq.answer_de: Manhattan-Fahren ist stressig und teuer—Brücken-/Tunnelmaut, wenig Straßenparken, aggressive Ticketierung. Kein Rechts bei Rot ohne Schild. Viele Besucher nutzen ÖPNV, Taxi oder parken in äußeren Bezirken.

faq.question: What should I know when renting a car in the US?
faq.question_de: Was sollte ich beim Mietwagen in den USA beachten?
faq.answer: Credit card usually required; under-25 surcharge common. Confirm toll transponder (E-ZPass) policy and cross-state rules. Non-English licences benefit from an IDP. Inspect insurance coverage for liability limits.
faq.answer_de: Kreditkarte meist nötig; Zuschlag unter 25 üblich. Maut-Transponder (E-ZPass) und bundesstaatübergreifende Regeln klären. Nicht-englische Führerscheine profitieren von IDP. Versicherungsschutz prüfen.

source_entries.title: National Highway Traffic Safety Administration
source_entries.notes: Federal authority for road safety standards, school bus rules, and national guidance.

source_entries.title: U.S. Department of Transportation
source_entries.notes: Federal transport department with policy and safety resources.

source_entries.title: California DMV - Driver handbook
source_entries.notes: Official California driver handbook and state-specific rules including phone laws.

source_entries.title: Florida Highway Safety and Motor Vehicles
source_entries.notes: Official Florida driver licensing and traffic law reference.

source_entries.title: Governors Highway Safety Association
source_entries.notes: State-by-state summaries of traffic laws including phone, seat belt, and DUI policies.

summary: Rules are state-based but generally consistent. 'Right on Red' is standard (stop first) unless signed otherwise. Stop signs are meant literally. Do not pass stopped school buses.
common_traps: Passing a school bus with lights flashing (Critical offense).
Move Over Law: You must change lanes for stopped emergency/service vehicles.

speed_limits.notes: Varies wildly by state. Freeway limits range from 65 to 85 mph. Watch signs.

alcohol_limit.notes: Stricter (0.04% or less) for commercial drivers. Open container laws are strict.

rules.seatbelt_rules: Mandatory in almost all states (Click it or Ticket).
rules.child_seat_rules: State laws vary but generally <8 years/booster seat required.
rules.phone_usage_rules: Texting banned in 48 states. Hands-free laws vary (many states ban holding phone).
rules.headlights_rules: Sunset to Sunrise. Wipers On = Lights On (in most states).
rules.priority_rules: 4-Way Stop: First to stop = First to go. If unclear, yield to right. Yellow school bus flashing = STOP.

tolls.notes: Turnpikes in East/Midwest. Bridges/Tunnels in NYC/SF. Plate readers or E-ZPass often used.

rules.parking_rules: Red curb = Fire lane. Colored curbs mean restrictions. Park in direction of travel.
rules.winter_rules: Tire chains required in Rockies/Sierra Nevada passes during storms.

summaries.title: Speeding
summaries.summary: Limits are posted in mph and enforced by radar, lidar, and cameras. Penalties vary sharply by state and whether the violation is in a school or work zone.
summaries.summary_de: Limits sind in mph ausgeschildert und werden per Radar, Lidar und Kameras kontrolliert. Strafen variieren stark nach Bundesstaat und Schul-/Baustellenzone.

summaries.title: Mobile phone while driving
summaries.summary: Texting while driving is banned in nearly all states. Many states now prohibit holding a phone; hands-free mounting is required where signed.
summaries.summary_de: SMS am Steuer ist in fast allen Bundesstaaten verboten. Viele verbieten Handy in der Hand; Freisprech-Montage ist wo ausgeschildert Pflicht.

summaries.title: Red light violation
summaries.summary: Red-light cameras operate in many cities. Running a red after a full stop violation at school zones carries extra penalties.
summaries.summary_de: Rotlichtkameras sind in vielen Städten im Einsatz. Rotlicht in Schulzonen bringt zusätzliche Strafen.

summaries.title: Alcohol offenses
summaries.summary: 0.08% BAC is the standard limit for private drivers. DUI triggers criminal charges, ignition interlocks, and licence suspension in most states.
summaries.summary_de: 0,08 % BAC ist Standard für Privatfahrer. DUI führt in den meisten Bundesstaaten zu Strafverfahren, Wegfahrsperre und Entzug.

categories.title: Speeding
categories.description: Typical base penalties; school zones, work zones, and reckless driving thresholds add surcharges.
categories.description_de: Typische Basisstrafen; Schul-, Baustellen- und Rücksichtslosigkeits-Schwellen erhöhen die Strafen.

categories.title: School Bus & Move Over
categories.description: Critical US-specific rules with severe penalties in most states.
categories.description_de: Wichtige US-spezifische Regeln mit schweren Strafen in den meisten Bundesstaaten.

rows.label: Passing stopped school bus (red lights flashing)

rental_and_idp_notes: Credit card usually mandatory. Under 25 fee applies. IDP recommended for non-English licenses.

differences.phone_usage_rules: Strict hands-free. Phone must be mounted.

regional_variations.notes: Lane splitting by motorcycles is legal. immense traffic in LA/SF.

highlights.title: Hands-free phone law
highlights.description: Holding a phone while driving is illegal. Mount your device and use voice commands or single-swipe gestures only.

highlights.title: Motorcycle lane splitting
highlights.description: Motorcycles may filter between lanes in slow traffic when done safely—unique among US states.

highlights.title: HOV and express lanes
highlights.description: Carpool lanes and paid express lanes are common in LA and Bay Area. FasTrak transponder may be required.

differences.priority_rules: NO Right on Red in NYC limits unless sign permits.

regional_variations.notes: Don't drive in Manhattan if you can avoid it. Expensive tolls and parking.

highlights.title: No right on red
highlights.description: Turning right at red lights is prohibited in NYC unless a sign explicitly allows it—unlike most of the US.

highlights.title: Bridge and tunnel tolls
highlights.description: Crossing into Manhattan often requires toll payment via E-ZPass or bill-by-mail. Budget for daily costs.

highlights.title: Alternate side parking
highlights.description: Street parking rules rotate for street cleaning. Misreading signs leads to quick tickets and towing.

regional_variations.notes: Headlights must be on if it is raining. Move Over law strictly enforced.

highlights.title: Headlights in rain
highlights.description: State law requires headlights when wipers run continuously in rain, fog, or smoke.

highlights.title: Move Over enforcement
highlights.description: Florida strictly enforces moving over for stopped emergency and service vehicles on highways.

highlights.title: SunPass toll roads
highlights.description: Many Florida highways use electronic tolling. Rental cars may include transponders—confirm fees.
